query: 2026 European Aquatics Junior Swimming Championships Munich July 7-12 2026 detailed event schedule. How many individual and relay gold-medal final events are contested on each day - day 1 (July 7), day 2 (July 8), day 3 (July 9) versus days 4-6? What is the total number of gold-medal events over the full 6-day meet?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary answering your question:

## 2026 European Aquatics Junior Swimming Championships — Munich (July 7–12, 2026)

**Gold-medal finals per day:**

| Day | Date | Total Golds | Individual | Relay |
|-----|------|-------------|-----------|-------|
| Day 1 | July 7 | **3** | 1 | 2 |
| Day 2 | July 8 | **8** | 7 | 1 (mixed) |
| Day 3 | July 9 | **6** | 5 | 1 |
| Day 4 | July 10 | **10** | 9 | 1 (mixed) |
| Day 5 | July 11 | **5** | 4 | 1 |
| Day 6 | July 12 | **12** | 10 | 2 |

**Key answers:**
- **Day 1 (July 7): 3 golds** — the fewest of the meet. Only one individual final (Girls 400 IM) plus the two 4×100 free relays; everything else was semifinals.
- **Day 2 (July 8): 8 golds** (7 individual + Mixed 4×100 Free Relay).
- **Day 3 (July 9): 6 golds** (5 individual + Girls 4×200 Free Relay).
- **Days 1–3 combined: 17 golds** vs. **Days 4–6 combined: 27 golds** — the back half is markedly more medal-dense, peaking on the final day (12).
- **Full 6-day total: 44 gold-medal events** = **36 individual + 8 relay**.

**Data source/caveat:** All day-by-day figures come from SwimSwam's six live finals recaps (accessed July 13, 2026), which report the finals actually swum each evening. The official European Aquatics schedule page and February 2026 Information Bulletin PDF could not be text-extracted directly, but the SwimSwam recaps are internally consistent across all six days and match the standard European Junior Championships program structure.

key_facts: Meet: 2026 European Aquatics Junior Swimming Championships, Munich, Germany, July 7-12, 2026, LCM (50m), 6 days.
Day 1 (July 7): 3 gold-medal finals = 1 individual (Girls 400 IM) + 2 relays (Girls & Boys 4x100 Free Relay).
Day 2 (July 8): 8 gold-medal finals = 7 individual + 1 mixed relay (Mixed 4x100 Free Relay, World Junior Record).
Day 3 (July 9): 6 gold-medal finals = 5 individual + 1 relay (Girls 4x200 Free Relay).
Days 1-3 combined: 17 gold-medal events (13 individual + 4 relay).
Day 4 (July 10): 10 gold-medal finals = 9 individual + 1 mixed relay (Mixed 4x100 Medley Relay).
Day 5 (July 11): 5 gold-medal finals = 4 individual + 1 relay (Boys 4x200 Free Relay, European Junior Record).
Day 6 (July 12): 12 gold-medal finals = 10 individual + 2 relays (Girls & Boys 4x100 Medley Relay).
Days 4-6 combined: 27 gold-medal events (23 individual + 4 relay).
Full 6-day total: 44 gold-medal events = 36 individual + 8 relay.
Day 1 has the fewest golds of the meet (3) due to a semifinal-heavy opening session; Day 6 has the most (12).
Eight relay finals total: 2x 4x100 free (D1), mixed 4x100 free (D2), girls 4x200 free (D3), mixed 4x100 medley (D4), boys 4x200 free (D5), girls + boys 4x100 medley (D6).
